meta.setting: French Gulch, Clear Creek, Shasta, and the Jefferson State mountain roads
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with horseback travel, camp pressure, law heat, debt, and rough moral choice

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Clear Creek gold rush movement, road settlement, and frontier communities.

research_grounding.title: NPS The Wintu
research_grounding.use: Clear Creek as Wintu homeland and seasonal lifeway, keeping water knowledge grounded and respectful.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Ford Men cast page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON gives shape and direction only; do not treat it as final page prose.
page_shape: square image reference immediately after the title
opening blockquote with invented work-note, toll warning, or road saying generated later
H2 archetype subtitle in the register of Crossing Hands
one or two compact lore paragraphs introducing the group as creek-crossing laborers, toll hagglers, and practical judges of wagon risk
H3 Role with a short table-facing function phrase
H3 Traits with two to four terse signs or habits
10 H4 headings, each followed later by a brief playable move, leverage, cost, edge, or complication
H3 Camp Say closing blockquote with plain meaning generated later
generation_guidance: Make them working people who know Clear Creek by sound, depth, hoof panic, and who is trying to cross before sunrise.
Tie their future moves to safe crossing, toll leverage, hidden cargo, rescue, witness memory, washed-out roads, and flood risk.
Give them rough humor, sore backs, and professional pride; do not make water knowledge mystical or ceremonial.
Let the page create play at a ford: negotiation, danger, timing, debts, lies, and the choice to help somebody who cannot pay.
Do not include final move names or final move text in this JSON.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: They should feel like men who can tell a liar by how he lets his horse drink. The ford is a courtroom with current under it.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A crossing scene should ask what the riders are carrying, who wants them delayed, and whether the water is kinder than the people on the bank.
must_preserve: Keep the group practical, human, and socially useful.
The generated page must include image path cast/images/ford-men.png.
No finished page prose, final moves, or final sayings belong in this prompt file.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not include medieval, enchanted, sacred, or fake-tribal framing
do not turn the ford into scenery only; it must generate choices and costs
do not use modern slang, modern gear, vehicles, or clean theme-park staging
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about ford labor, crossing judgment, road power, and table action.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
